answer:
Sketch:
- Acquired 2016 → post-9/27/2010 → 100% QSBS exclusion
- Cap: greater of $10M or 10× basis; $8M < $10M
- 100%-exclusion shares: no AMT preference, no NIIT
- Held >5 years ✓

Answer: Roughly **$0 federal tax**. Your $8M gain falls entirely under the §1202 100% exclusion (shares acquired after Sept 27, 2010, held 5+ years, under the $10M cap), and 100%-excluded gain is also exempt from AMT and the 3.8% NIIT. Just confirm the company met QSBS requirements at issuance (C-corp, <$50M gross assets, qualified trade or business, original issuance to you) — get a QSBS attestation letter from the company and have your CPA document it. State tax may still apply (e.g., California doesn't conform).
